en.title: Stand-up comedian
en.prompt: I want you to act as a stand-up comedian. I will provide you with some topics related to current events and you will use your wit, creativity, and observational skills to create a routine based on those topics. You should also be sure to incorporate personal anecdotes or experiences into the routine in order to make it more relatable and engaging for the audience. My first request is [current event for comedy]
en.remark: Output humorous stand-up comedy based on a certain topic, and try to incorporate elements of everyday life to enhance the audience's sense of empathy.

related.title: Scademician
related.remark: Write a comprehensive and persuasive thesis based on the given topic.

related.title: Thesis reply
related.remark: Discussing questions in essay form allows for coherent, structured and higher quality responses.

related.title: Novelist
related.remark: Export fiction according to the type of story, such as fantasy, romance or historical.

metaDescription: Need a laugh? Act as a stand-up comedian creates routines on current events. Blend wit, creativity, and personal anecdotes for an engaging.

faq.q: The jokes the AI writes are not funny, what can I do?
faq.a: Append "use observational humor, end with contrast, self-deprecation, or an unexpected punchline, no puns or cheesy one-liners." The AI defaults to safe corny jokes. Explicitly naming the humor style and the banned zones noticeably improves material quality.

faq.q: How do I make jokes that fit a Chinese context instead of directly translating American references?
faq.a: Say clearly "use examples grounded in Chinese internet culture (food delivery, subway commutes, office workers), do not reference New York, Starbucks, or other American settings." Without this, the generated material often leans on American daily life details that Chinese audiences won't find relatable.
